title: syzygium micranthum
intro: Syzygium micranthum, also known as the Malabar ironwood or Malabar karingali, is a plant species that belongs to the family Myrtaceae and is native to the tropical regions of the Western Ghats in India. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Syzygium micranthum include its ability to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and wounds. Syzygium micranthum is traditionally prepared in the form of herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are made by boiling the leaves and bark of the plant in water.
